perturbation.original_text:
(a) GOVERNING LAW. THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNED BY, AND CONSTRUED IN ACCORDANCE WITH, THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF COLORADO, UNITED STATES, WITHOUT REGARD TO ITS PRINCIPLES REGARDING CONFLICT OF LAWS.
(b) ARBITRATION. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including, without limitation, the interpretation of any provision of this Agreement or the breach, termination or invalidity of this Agreement that cannot reasonably be resolved by the Parties shall be settled exclusively and finally by binding arbitration under the International Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association in effect on and as of the date of this Agreement (the "AAA Rules"), except as such AAA Rules are modified pursuant to this Section 10.12(b).
perturbation.changed_text:
(a) GOVERNING LAW. THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNED BY, AND CONSTRUED IN ACCORDANCE WITH, THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF COLORADO, UNITED STATES, WITHOUT REGARD TO ITS PRINCIPLES REGARDING CONFLICT OF LAWS.
(b) ARBITRATION. Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including, without limitation, the interpretation of any provision of this Agreement or the breach, termination or invalidity of this Agreement that cannot reasonably be resolved by the Parties will have non-binding arbitration if all parties want it. All other methods will be acceptable as well.
perturbation.explanation: The arbitration clause is moved and made non-binding, contradicting the original intention of mandatory, binding arbitration. It also does not clearly state the arbitration process.
perturbation.law_explanation: CRS § 13-22-206 requires agreements to arbitrate to be valid, enforceable, and irrevocable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract. By making arbitration non-binding and subject to the agreement of all parties, the clause undermines the enforceability of the arbitration agreement as contemplated by Colorado law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the binding nature of the arbitration agreement by making it non-binding and contingent on the agreement of all parties. The law explanation states that CRS § 13-22-206 requires agreements to arbitrate to be valid, enforceable, and irrevocable. The modified text directly contradicts this by allowing for non-binding arbitration, thus weakening the enforceability of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 10.8 INDEMNIFICATION. Reseller shall be solely responsible for, and shall indemnify TouchStar, its officers, directors, employees, and agents against, and hold each of them harmless from, any and all claims (including without limitation, all damages (whether direct, indirect, incidental, criminal, special, or punitive), losses, liabilities, expenses, costs, and attorneys' fees related to such claims) resulting from (a) the negligent or willful failure of Reseller to comply with its obligations hereunder, (b) the acts or omissions of Reseller, its officers, directors, employees, or agents during the term of this Agreement or thereafter, (c) any express or implied representation or warranty made by Reseller or any of its officers, directors, employees or agents with regard to the TouchStar Software or the Support Services not contained in written literature of TouchStar or specifically authorized by TouchStar in writing, and (c) the installation of a call center system by TouchStar on behalf of Reseller or any Other Reseller and the use of the TouchStar Software in the operation of a call center system, unless caused by the gross negligence or willful misconduct of TouchStar.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.8 INDEMNIFICATION. Reseller shall be solely responsible for, and shall indemnify TouchStar, its officers, directors, employees, and agents against, and hold each of them harmless from, any and all claims resulting from (a) the negligent or willful failure of Reseller to comply with its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The indemnification clause is severely curtailed, removing protection for TouchStar from liability arising from Reseller's representations, warranties and call center installation except if there is gross negligence or willful misconduct. This introduces a legal contradiction as it alters the scope of indemnification.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Colorado law, indemnification agreements are generally enforceable but are strictly construed against the indemnitee (TouchStar). By significantly narrowing the scope of indemnification to exclude claims arising from Reseller's representations or call center installations unless gross negligence or willful misconduct is proven, it increases TouchStar's exposure to liability in scenarios where the Reseller has been negligent. This contradicts the original intention of broader protection for TouchStar.

perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly reduces the scope of indemnification for TouchStar, removing protections against claims arising from the Reseller's representations, warranties, and call center installations. This contradicts the original contract's intent of broader protection. Although the provided snippets do not contain law that indicates whether this is permissible or not, the definition of 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' states that a legal contradiction arises when the organization of a contract affects its clarity or enforceability. Removing significant portions of the indemnification clause falls under affecting the clarity and enforceability of the contract, creating a legal contradiction under the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1 RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES. Neither party to this Agreement and none of their respective agents, employees, representatives or independent contractors shall (a) be considered an agent, employee, or representative of the other party for any purpose whatsoever, (b) have any authority to make any agreement or commitment for the other party or to incur liability or obligation in the other party's name or on its behalf, or (c) represent to third parties that any of them has any right so to bind the other party hereto, it being intended that each party shall remain an independent contractor responsible only for its own actions. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed or interpreted as creating an agency, partnership, or joint venture relationship between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES. Notwithstanding the independent contractor clause, Reseller will have the authority to make any agreement or commitment for TouchStar, should the situation arise.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause that Reseller will have the authority to make any agreement or commitment for TouchStar, it introduces contradiction within the same paragraph. This fundamentally alters the nature of the relationship and creates ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Agency law under Colorado common law
perturbation.law_explanation: Under Colorado common law on agency, the determination of whether an agency relationship exists depends on the facts and circumstances of each case, focusing on the principal's right to control the agent's conduct. The modified clause directly contradicts the establishment of an independent contractor relationship by granting the Reseller authority to act on behalf of TouchStar, which implies the existence of an agency relationship. The agent must be faithful to his trust, must use reasonable care and skill, and must act only in accordance with the principal's instructions.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes an independent contractor relationship, explicitly denying any agency relationship. The changed text introduces a clause granting the Reseller authority to make agreements or commitments for TouchStar, which directly contradicts the independent contractor status. The Law Explanation supports this, stating that granting such authority implies the existence of an agency relationship. The changed text undermines the original intent of an independent contractor relationship by granting agency-like authority, creating a direct and impactful legal contradiction based on the explanation.
